title: Ensure that the --bind-address argument is set to 127.0.0.1

input_row.title: Ensure that the --bind-address argument is set to 127.0.0.1
input_row.description: Do not bind the scheduler service to non-loopback insecure addresses.
input_row.rationale: The Scheduler API service which runs on port 10251/TCP by default is used for health and metrics information and is available without authentication or encryption. As such it should only be bound to a localhost interface, to minimize the cluster's attack surface
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the master node: ps -ef | grep kube-scheduler Verify that the --bind-address argument is set to 127.0.0.1
input_row.remediation: Edit the Scheduler pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- scheduler.yaml on the master node and ensure the correct value for the --bind- address parameter
input_row.default_value: By default, the --bind-address parameter is set to 0.0.0.0

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_scheduler_bind_address_set_to_loopback

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the configuration of the kube-scheduler.
- The specific resource to enumerate is the kube-scheduler pod manifest located at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-scheduler.yaml`.
- The command to retrieve the kube-scheduler configuration is:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pod -n kube-system kube-scheduler-<scheduler-pod-name> -o yaml
  ```
- Validate the `--bind-address` argument in the command line arguments section of the pod specification.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: The `--bind-address` argument is set to `127.0.0.1`.
  - Fail: The `--bind-address` argument is set to any value other than `127.0.0.1` (e.g., `0.0.0.0` or any external IP).
- Example: Check the output of the command for the presence of the `--bind-address=127.0.0.1` setting in the args section.
